name: How do I file a claim for respiratory damage from workplace fumes?
description: Notify employer/insurer in writing, submit medical evidence to comp board, expect evaluation.

mainEntity.name: How do I file a claim for respiratory damage from workplace fumes?

acceptedAnswer.text: Notify employer/insurer in writing, submit medical evidence to comp board, expect evaluation.
acceptedAnswer.description: **Start by notifying your employer in writing within state deadlines, then file with workers' comp board.** Process involves medical evaluation proving occupational cause, like diacetyl or silica exposure[2][4]. Key factors: Submit job history, exposure details, doctor reports; hearings resolve disputes; appeals possible. Utah requires filing within 3 years of injury knowledge. Use certified mail for notice, request air quality records from employer, track all communications. Legal help navigates denials common in delayed-symptom cases.
